title: Brs Dry Bulk Weekly Newsletter
summary:
A different perspective on China's surprising surge in coal imports
According to Chinese Customs, China imported 261.18 mln mt of coal (via all transportation modes, seaborne, railway and truck included) in the first seven months of 2023, up 88.6% year on year. If this import pace is maintained across the remainder of the year, China's annual coal imports could exceed a record 400 mln mt this year. This significant import growth is undoubtedly driven by an improved appetite for coal following China exiting its pandemic restrictions, that being said, this staggering increment in volumes (in absolute and relative terms) has taken many by surprise.
